   - Note-taking tool

Let me think about the design:

- Use `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` for the chat client abstraction
- Use `AIFunction` and `AIFunctionFactory` to create tools
- The web search tool will perform actual web searches (or simulate them if no API key)


- The note-taking tool will maintain a collection of notes that the agent can reference
- The agent loop will repeatedly call the LLM until it determines the research is complete

Since we don't have guaranteed API keys, I'll design it to read from environment variables and provide clear setup instructions. I'm leaning toward using `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` patterns with `OpenAIChatClient` or similar abstractions, though I need to verify what's available in the `Microsoft.Agents.AI` package.

Looking at the available packages, I can use `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` for the core abstractions like `IChatClient`, `ChatMessage`, and `AIFunction`, with `OpenAI` providing the underlying client. I'll structure the implementation with a static tools class containing the web search and note-taking functions, then build the main agent loop around that. For web search, I'll either use a real HTTP-based approach like DuckDuckGo's instant answer API or create a clean simulation if API keys aren't available. I'm settling on DuckDuckGo's API for the search tool since it doesn't require authentication, then I'll add the note-taking functions and wire everything into an agent loop using what `Microsoft.Agents.AI` provides.

Setting up the search tool... Setting up the OpenAI client with environment variables for the API key and model selection, then configuring it with automatic function invocation...

Building out the tools for the research agent...

Parsing the DuckDuckGo lite HTML results using regex patterns to extract links and snippets, then collecting the top 5 results with their URLs and content. Parsing the DuckDuckGo API response to extract the abstract text and URL, then adding it as a summary result if available, and iterating through related topics to gather additional search results. Fetching the HTML and cleaning it up... Finishing the note retrieval logic and registering all the tools I've created. Then I'm prompting the user for a research topic and validating their input before starting the research process.

Now I'm setting up the agent with system instructions that outline the research workflow—searching the web, fetching pages, saving findings as notes, and eventually summarizing everything with proper citations. The user message is beginning to ask the agent to start researching the provided topic.

I'm also noticing there's a consideration about return statements in top-level programs that I need to handle carefully.

Let me verify the middleware setup — `UseFunctionInvocation()` should be available on the `ChatClientBuilder` and will automatically manage the tool invocation loop, feeding results back to the model. I'm checking the package versions to ensure everything aligns: `Microsoft.Agents.AI` at `1.*-*`, `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` at `9.*`, and `OpenAI` at `2.*`. The OpenAI package should transitively bring in the necessary `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` dependencies.

I need to confirm the exact method names for the chat client API — it might be `GetResponseAsync()` or `CompleteAsync()` rather than `GetChatResponseAsync()`, and I should verify how to properly convert the OpenAI client to an `IChatClient` using the appropriate extension method. Let me write the code and do a restore to see what's actually available, then iterate from there.

<member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory">
            <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> class creates <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances that wrap .NET methods
            (specified as <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>). As part of this process, JSON schemas are
            automatically derived for both the function's input parameters (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>)
            and, by default, the function's return type (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>).
            These schemas are produced using the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> and
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>, and enable AI services to understand and
            interact with the function. Return value serialization and schema derivation behavior can be customized
            via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
            respectively.
            </para>
            </remarks>
            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory._defaultOptions">
            <summary>Holds the default options instance used when creating function.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory.Create(System.Delegate,Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions)">
            <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via a delegate.</summary>
            <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</param>
            <param name="options">Metadata to use to override defaults inferred from <paramref name="method"/>.</param>
            <returns>The created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            By default, any parameters to <paramref name="method"/> are sourced from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary
            of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:
            <list type="bullet">
              <item>
                <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to the <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> passed into
                the invocation via <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>'s <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameter. The parameter is
                not included in the generated JSON schema. The behavior of <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters can't be overridden.
              </item>
              <item>
                By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
                and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
                <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>
                must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.

data.content:
Build succeeds with zero errors/warnings, and the error-handling path works correctly. Here's what was built:

**ResearchAgent** — a .NET 10 console app with an AI-powered agentic research loop using `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`.

### Tools available to the agent:
| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `SearchWeb` | Queries DuckDuckGo (lite HTML + instant-answer API fallback) |
| `FetchWebPage` | Fetches & extracts visible text from any URL |
| `SaveNote` | Stores titled research notes in memory |
| `GetAllNotes` | Retrieves all collected notes |

### How it works:
1. The `FunctionInvokingChatClient` middleware auto-executes tool calls in a loop
2. The system prompt instructs the agent to search → read pages → save notes → summarize
3. The final LLM response is a cited, comprehensive summary

### Usage:
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
export OPENAI_MODEL="gpt-4o-mini"   # optional
dotnet run
# → Enter a research topic: quantum computing
```
